talk_title: CUPIA 2 collaborative multi-agent LLM for cancer of unknown primary diagnosis

models.name: CUPIA 2 LLM framework
models.genotype_or_key_features: NGS mutation profiles across 19 cancer types

MOA_summary: CUPIA 2 uses a multi-agent large language model ensemble to ingest NGS variant data and generate tissue-of-origin predictions with rationale derived from a 100,000-patient Smart Data database, achieving ~77% top‑1 accuracy internally and ~62% on independent MSK data.
key_findings: CUPIA 2 achieved 77% top‑1 accuracy and 93% top‑3 accuracy across 19 cancer types in internal validation.
On independent MSK cohort (12,000 samples) top‑1 accuracy was 62% and top‑3 accuracy 82%.
Model provided rationale linking co‑mutations (e.g., KRAS G12D + TP53 R175H) to predicted tissue origins.
Chairman LLM overwrote 24% of ensemble predictions during training.
open_questions: How to improve performance on under‑represented cancer types such as small‑cell lung cancer and endometrial cancer?
Can multimodal inputs (RNA‑seq, imaging) be incorporated to boost accuracy?

talk_title: Deep learning model predicts palbociclib response in ER+ HER2‑ negative advanced breast cancer

speaker.name: Akshat Singhal
speaker.affiliation: City of Hope / UC San Diego

targets.dependency_context: ER+ HER2‑ negative advanced breast cancer

models.name: PalboVNN
models.genotype_or_key_features: trained on 900 cell lines, 718 gene panel

models.name: UCSD cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 139 patients, NGS within ±180 days of palbociclib start

MOA_summary: A visible neural network maps genomic alterations to hierarchical protein complexes, enabling prediction of palbociclib sensitivity; the model stratifies patients into sensitive (HR 0.38, median PFS +14 mo) and resistant groups, with specificity for CDK4/6 inhibition versus endocrine therapy alone.
key_findings: PalboVNN stratified UCSD patients (n=36) into responders vs non‑responders with HR 0.38 and median PFS benefit of 14 months.
In the larger MSK cohort (n=474) the model retained significance (HR 0.56) with an 18‑month median PFS advantage.
Model performance was specific to palbociclib‑containing regimens and did not stratify letrozole‑only patients.
Interpretation identified 18 protein assemblies (11 shared) driving predictions, including DNA integrity and AKT signaling.
A prospective phase‑II trial has been launched based on these findings.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: ROS1 mutation was a dominant driver of predicted resistance in a non‑responding patient.
open_questions: Can the VNN framework be generalized to other CDK4/6 inhibitors (ribociclib, abemaciclib) with comparable performance?
How will inclusion of clinical covariates (ancestry, comorbidities) affect model accuracy?

talk_title: AI‑derived H&E biomarkers predict survival under immunotherapy vs chemotherapy in NEPTUNE NSCLC trial

biomarkers.name: AI‑derived fibroblast density
biomarkers.finding: high fibroblast density associated with worse OS in both IO and chemo arms

biomarkers.name: AI‑derived immune cell density
biomarkers.finding: high immune density predicted better OS in IO arm but not in chemo arm

models.name: NEPTUNE phase III NSCLC
models.genotype_or_key_features: 468 H&E images (426 after QC), IO vs chemo arms

clinical_data.value: significant separation by median split of immune phenotype in IO arm

MOA_summary: PathAI's off‑the‑shelf AI model segments tumor, immune and fibroblast compartments on H&E slides, quantifies densities and spatial relationships, and these features improve survival stratification beyond PD‑L1 and TMB, especially predicting benefit from immunotherapy.
key_findings: Fibroblast density was a negative prognostic marker in both IO and chemo arms (high density → worse OS).
Immune cell density showed a predictive interaction: high density correlated with improved OS only in the immunotherapy arm.
Multivariate Cox model adding H&E features to PD‑L1/TMB achieved significant separation (p<0.05) whereas clinical features alone did not.
At 18‑month follow‑up, high‑risk group defined by H&E features had 21% event rate, supporting enrichment potential.
open_questions: Will prospective validation confirm the added predictive value of H&E‑derived biomarkers?
Can these AI features be integrated with other omics (e.g., RNA‑seq) for further improvement?

talk_title: Deep learning predicts metabolic pathway activity from routine H&E slides

models.name: ResNet34‑decoder metabolic pathway predictor
models.genotype_or_key_features: Tile‑level H&E images labeled with binary pathway activity from GSMMs

MOA_summary: A ResNet34 encoder‑decoder network is trained on 256 × 256 H&E tiles paired with binary metabolic pathway activity derived from patient‑specific genome‑scale metabolic models (GSMMs) built from TCGA transcriptomics, enabling prediction of pathway enrichment directly from morphology.
key_findings: Across 21 cancer types, 87% of 450 pathway‑cancer models achieved AUC > 0.5.
9% of models (top 10 pathways) reached AUC 0.65–0.85, notably nucleotide metabolism (AUC 0.85 in TGCT) and nuclear transport (AUC 0.79 in COAD).
Metabolic pathways with high predictability clustered in nucleotide synthesis, nuclear/mitochondrial transport, and fatty‑acid β‑oxidation.
Demonstrated feasibility of using H&E morphology to infer functional metabolic states, suggesting utility for trial enrichment.
open_questions: Will external validation on independent cohorts confirm the high‑performing pathways?
Can predicted metabolic activity be linked to response to pathway‑targeted therapies?

talk_title: Longitudinal fusion of low‑dose CT improves lung cancer risk prediction

speaker.name: Hanieh Ajami
speaker.affiliation: Moffitt Cancer Center / University of South Florida

models.name: Hybrid, Bi‑fusion, Tri‑fusion architectures
models.genotype_or_key_features: Sybil CNN features from up to three LDCT time points

MOA_summary: Multiple low‑dose CT scans are processed by the Sybil model to extract encoded and attention features; these are fused via vision and temporal transformers (Hybrid) or concatenated (Bi/ Tri‑fusion) to produce six‑year risk scores, leveraging temporal disease progression for improved discrimination.
key_findings: Hybrid architecture achieved near‑perfect AUC (0.99) at year 1 and maintained 0.94 at year 6 on NLST test set.
Hybrid model outperformed Bi‑fusion and Tri‑fusion across all horizons and also generalized to external Moffitt cohort after fine‑tuning.
Statistical tests confirmed significant performance differences (p < 0.05) between hybrid and other models.
Model handles missing time points via attention masking, making it applicable to real‑world screening data.
open_questions: How will incorporation of clinical/demographic risk factors affect long‑term prediction?
Can federated learning across institutions improve robustness to variable scan intervals?

talk_title: Multimodal deep learning predicts gastric cancer response to first‑line anti‑PD‑1 therapy

biomarkers.name: CRP multimodal model prediction
biomarkers.finding: predicted partial response associated with longer PFS (HR 0.45) and OS (HR 0.48) across training, validation, and two external cohorts

models.name: CRT multimodal model
models.genotype_or_key_features: CT radiomics + H&E pathomics (CLIME + CNN‑Transformer) + clinical features

MOA_summary: A self‑attention fusion network combines CT‑derived radiomic features, H&E‑derived graph‑based pathomic embeddings, and clinical variables to predict first‑line chemo‑immunotherapy response; high predicted PR status correlates with increased tumor fraction, up‑regulated immune‑activating pathways, and improved survival.
key_findings: CRT model achieved AUC 0.97 for response prediction in training set and validated across two external cohorts.
Predicted PR patients had significantly longer PFS and OS (HR ≈ 0.45–0.48) after multivariate adjustment for age, sex, PD‑L1 CPS.
Pathway analysis showed enrichment of immune‑activating signatures (alpha/gamma response) in predicted PR group versus immune‑inhibitory pathways (IL‑10, NF‑κB) in non‑PR group.
Model outperformed PD‑L1 CPS and unimodal radiomics or pathomics models in precision‑recall and confusion matrix metrics.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: High fibroblast density (from pathomics) was linked to non‑PR status and immune‑inhibitory gene expression.
open_questions: Will prospective multicenter trials confirm the CRT model’s predictive power for treatment selection?
Can the model be adapted for other immunotherapy regimens or combination therapies?

company_or_sponsor: Yangtze River Delta Guochi Intelligent Medical Technology Co.
